title: Make getGraphData() public (remove auth requirement)

requirements.text: In backend/main.mo, remove the { caller } binding and the AccessControl.hasPermission check from the getGraphData() query function so it is publicly accessible without authentication. The function signature should change from `public query ({ caller }) func getGraphData() : async GraphData` to `public query ({ }) func getGraphData() : async GraphData`, and the Runtime.trap("Unauthorized: ...") guard block should be deleted entirely.

quotes: Remove auth check from getGraphData() in backend
getGraphData() requires #user permission, but landing page uses anonymous actor
Remove the { caller } and the permission check entirely (lines 1718-1720)

acceptanceCriteria: getGraphData() is declared as `public query ({ }) func getGraphData() : async GraphData` with no caller binding
The AccessControl.hasPermission check and Runtime.trap call are removed from getGraphData()
An anonymous (unauthenticated) actor can successfully call getGraphData() without receiving an authorization error
The landing page's LandingGraphDiagram component can fetch graph data on first visit without a cached response

constraints: Only modify the getGraphData() function — do not alter any other access control checks in backend/main.mo
nonGoals: Making any other backend endpoints public
Modifying the landing page cache behavior
